title: Wheel Rotation Health Check — 6-checkpoint verifier that confirms the Navigator→AP→Hub loop is actually closing
what: A post-Autopilot verification script (tools/wheel_rotation_health_check.py) that runs 6 timestamped checks against file artifacts to confirm the full Navigator→AP→Hub intelligence loop completed correctly. On any checkpoint failure, it writes a bug lug to WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/bug/open/ naming the failing checkpoint and broken link. Outputs rotation-check-latest.json with pass/fail per checkpoint and a loop_closed boolean. Can be invoked manually or wired to a post-AP hook.
why: The 5 broken links (L1-L5) in the wheel turning loop are invisible during normal operation — everything appears to 'run fine' even when AP ignores Navigator, or Hub never processes completion events. Without explicit timestamped checks that verify each link closed, the wheel can silently degrade back to broken state after any Basher update or config change. The health check makes the loop's closure a measurable, auditable property — not an assumption.

checkpoints.name: navigator_profile_loaded
checkpoints.source: AP run log (last entry in WAI-Spoke/advisors/ozi/autopilot-log.jsonl or --ap-log flag)
checkpoints.pass_condition: AP log entry has navigator_profile_used key with non-null value AND generated_at of recommendations-current.json is within 48h of AP run start_ts
checkpoints.failure_message: Autopilot did not load Navigator profile — L1 (Navigator↔AP) still broken

checkpoints.name: phase1_teachings_ran
checkpoints.pass_condition: AP log entry has teachings_adopted key (value 0 is acceptable — key absence means Phase 1 is still a stub)
checkpoints.failure_message: AP Phase 1 teachings key absent from log — L2 (Phase 1 stub) still broken

checkpoints.name: completion_event_emitted
checkpoints.pass_condition: At least one .json file in that dir has completed_at >= AP run start_ts AND spoke_id matches this spoke
checkpoints.failure_message: No completion event found in hub for this AP run — L3 (Phase 5 emit) still broken

checkpoints.name: hub_advisory_refresh_ran
checkpoints.pass_condition: Log has at least one entry with processed_at >= AP run start_ts
checkpoints.failure_message: Hub advisory refresh log has no entry since AP run — L5 (process_completion_events mocked) still broken

checkpoints.name: navigator_republished
checkpoints.pass_condition: recommendations-current.json.generated_at > V4 hub advisory refresh processed_at (Navigator ran AFTER hub refreshed)
checkpoints.failure_message: Navigator has not republished since last hub advisory refresh — full loop not closing

checkpoints.name: pathgraph_trace_written
checkpoints.pass_condition: At least one entry with ts >= AP run start_ts exists
checkpoints.failure_message: No PathGraph trace from this AP run — harness operational traces (impl-harness-traces-basher-v1) not yet implemented

how.trigger: Post-AP invocation: manual via CLI or wired to Stop hook after AP completes
steps: 1. Resolve spoke_path (--spoke-path arg or cwd), hub_path (--hub-path arg or read from WAI-State.json wheel.hub_path)
2. Find AP run log: try WAI-Spoke/advisors/ozi/autopilot-log.jsonl, fall back to --ap-log flag. Read last entry. Extract start_ts and spoke_id.
3. Run V1: load recommendations-current.json, check generated_at freshness. Load AP log last entry, check navigator_profile_used key present.
4. Run V2: check AP log last entry has teachings_adopted key (any value including 0).
5. Run V3: scan {hub_path}/WAI-Spoke/advisors/gardener/spoke-completion-events/*.json, find entry matching spoke_id with completed_at >= start_ts.
6. Run V4: read {hub_path}/WAI-Spoke/advisors/gardener/advisory-refresh-log.jsonl, find entry with processed_at >= start_ts.
7. Run V5: compare recommendations-current.json.generated_at to the V4 refresh timestamp. Pass if Navigator ran after refresh.
8. Run V6: scan WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl, find entry with ts >= start_ts.
9. Compute loop_closed: V1 AND V2 AND V3 AND V4 AND V5 all pass (V6 is advisory only — harness may not yet be wired).
11. If any core checkpoint (V1-V5) fails: write bug lug to WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/bug/open/bug-rotation-check-{checkpoint_id}-{yyyymmdd}.json.
12. Print human-readable summary to stdout. Exit 0 if loop_closed, exit 1 if not.
constraints: Never fail hard on missing files — missing AP log = skip all checks, write rotation-check-latest.json with error: no_ap_log_found
Missing hub_path = log warning, skip V3/V4/V5, do not fail the script
V6 failure does not set loop_closed=false — it is advisory (harness traces are separate from the wiring initiative)
Bug lugs must not be created on repeat runs for the same checkpoint failure on the same day — check if today's bug lug already exists before writing
Script is idempotent: safe to run multiple times after the same AP run

rotation_check_latest.description: Written after every health check run. Overwritten each run.

fields.run_ts: ISO-8601 timestamp of this health check run
fields.ap_run_start_ts: ISO-8601 timestamp from AP run log (null if log not found)
fields.loop_closed: boolean — V1 AND V2 AND V3 AND V4 AND V5 all true
broken_links: list of L-id strings for failed checkpoints
fields.error: null or string — populated if log not found or runtime error
advisory_only_failures: list of advisory checkpoint IDs that failed but don't affect loop_closed

fields.title: Wheel rotation checkpoint {checkpoint_id} failed: {failure_message}
fields.ap_run_start_ts: timestamp of the AP run that triggered the check

blocked_by_note: Verifier is meaningful only after all 5 broken links are wired (Waves 1-2 complete). The spec can be implemented in Wave 3.

tests.description: With a valid AP log showing navigator_profile_used, a completion event in hub dir, a fresh advisory-refresh entry, and Navigator timestamp > refresh timestamp — all 5 core checkpoints pass and loop_closed=true
With valid AP log but no matching completion event in hub dir — V3 fails, loop_closed=false, bug lug created for L3
When hub_path is not resolvable, V3/V4/V5 are skipped with warning — script exits without crash
With pathgraph/history.jsonl absent, V6 fails but loop_closed can still be true (V6 failure is advisory) — verify loop_closed not affected by V6
Running health check twice on same day with same failure does not create a second bug lug for that checkpoint

health.verification_note: All 6 checkpoints (V1-V6) pass. loop_closed=true confirmed. rotation-check-latest.json written. AP log path is WAI-Spoke/advisors/autopilot/activity-log.jsonl (run summaries + per-lug dispatch entries mixed). Hub path reads from WAI-State.json wheel.hub_path.

perceive: Read this spec in full. Read initiative-wheel-rotation-wiring-v1 to understand wave sequencing — this spec is Wave 3 (after Waves 1+2 complete). Read impl-wheel-rotation-health-check-v1 for implementation detail. Check open_questions in health section and resolve by reading ozi_autopilot.py Phase 5 output section before implementing.
execute: Do not implement until Wave 1 and Wave 2 of initiative-wheel-rotation-wiring-v1 are confirmed complete. When unblocked: create one impl lug (impl-wheel-rotation-health-check-v1 exists) and route to BASHER. Verifier must be pure Python stdlib only — no external dependencies.
verify: All 5 tests in tests[] pass. Run on a spoke where all 5 broken links are confirmed wired. rotation-check-latest.json shows loop_closed: true. Script exits 0.
